content:
The user is asking me to act as a "compact-strategy compactor" for the AlgebraicJacobian project. My job is to:

1. Read the instructions from `/home/archon/Lean_tests/AlgebraicJacobian/.archon/prompts/compactor-strategy.md`
2. Read the current STRATEGY.md file from `/home/archon/Lean_tests/AlgebraicJacobian/.archon/STRATEGY.md`
3. Rewrite STRATEGY.md in place to compress it while preserving all critical information
4. Report the compression results as one line at the end

Let me start by reading both files to understand what needs to be done.
I'll read the compactor instructions and the current STRATEGY.md file to understand what needs to be compressed.

1	# Strategy Compactor
2	
3	You shrink `.archon/STRATEGY.md` in place, ahead of the plan agent. You do NOT change the strategy, add ideas, or rewrite for style — only tighten old iteration-based content per the rules below.
4	
5	## Scope
6	
7	- **Write target**: `.archon/STRATEGY.md` only.
8	- **Do not read or write** any other file in `.archon/`, any `.lean` file, `blueprint/**`, or `archon-protected.yaml`. If a rule would make you reach for another file, stop — you are not the plan agent.
9	
10	## How to operate
11	
12	Default to **`Edit` calls**, not `Write`. Each shrinkable region (an old revision-log entry, an old iter-NNN paragraph anywhere in the file) is one targeted `Edit`. This is faster and leaves stable sections truly untouched.
13	
14	Use `Write` (full-file replace) **only** if your changes would require more than ~15 separate `Edit`s, or to insert the estimations table below into a file that doesn't have one yet.
15	
16	Do not compute the rewrite mentally first — read once, then act incrementally. Stop as soon as the rules below have nothing more to apply.
17	
18	Reply with one line at the end:
19	- `COMPACTED: <pre-size> -> <post-size> chars` on success
20	- `UNCHANGED: <one-sentence reason>` if no rule applied (and call no tools)
21	
22	## Preservation rules (non-negotiable)
23	
24	The plan agent reads STRATEGY.md to know the path to the end-state. **Never drop or summarize away:**
25	
26	- Mathlib gaps that are still open
27	- Errors / dead ends from prior iterations
28	- User decisions and authorisations
29	- Cross-references to specific files, line numbers, declarations
30	- Estimated effort numbers, even rough ones
31	- The file's section structure — heading hierarchy stays as you found it
32	
33	**When in doubt, KEEP.** Verbosity is cheap; lost knowledge is expensive.
34	
35	## What to compress
36	
37	Section names in STRATEGY.md are project-specific. Archon's template only seeds two headings (`# Strategy` and `## Revision log`); everything else is free-form prose the plan agent decides on per project. Identify shrinkable content by **shape**, not by section name.
38	
39	### Compress: iteration-tagged entries
40	
41	Any region whose entries are tagged with iter numbers (`iter NNN`, `(iter NNN)`, headings like `### iter-NNN`, bullets like `- iter NNN: …`). Typical hosts: the `## Revision log`, plus whatever "current state" / "where we sit" / "activity log" section a given project happens to maintain. Apply:
42	
43	- Find the largest iter number present anywhere in the file → call it `MAX`.
44	- Entries for iters in `[MAX-2, MAX]` (the last 3): **keep verbatim** — the plan agent will reason about these next.
45	- Entries for older iters: compress each to one bullet `- iter NNN — <one sentence>`. The sentence must record the **strategic change or outcome** (route abandoned, decomposition revised, effort estimate moved, Mathlib gap discovered, blocker resolved).
46	- Consecutive iters with identical strategic substance: merge into a range bullet `- iter NNN-MMM — strategy unchanged in substance; <one-sentence note>`.
47	- **Never drop an entry entirely.** Merged ranges still show their iter numbers.
48	
49	### Strip: iter-tagged notes that leaked into headings
50	
51	If a heading has accumulated inline iter notes like `### Step 1 — … (iter-020 done; iter-022 polish DONE; …)`, strip those parenthetical iter notes from the heading. If the same information isn't already in an iter-tagged section, leave a one-line bullet behind in the nearest iter-based section before stripping. The point is to keep headings stable while the iter history lives in the log.
52	
53	### Do not touch: stable strategic content
54	
55	Everything that's not iter-tagged — descriptions of the end-state, formal feasibility assessments, decomposition of the work, parallel-tracks discussion, refactors implied, prose introductions, any per-project framing the plan agent set up. Even if a paragraph looks verbose, the plan agent wrote it that way deliberately. Do not reorganize, rename, or merge sections.
56	
57	## Optional: top-of-file estimations table
58	
59	If the strategy already organises remaining work into named units (phases, steps, milestones, tracks, …), maintain a small summary table near the top so the plan agent can read effort at a glance. Insert it immediately after the first `# Strategy` heading, before any other section.
60	
61	```markdown
62	## Estimations (auto-maintained)
63	
64	| Unit | Iterations remaining (est.) | LOC remaining (est.) | Status |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| <name> | <iters> | <loc> | not started \| in progress (<short note>) \| blocked on <X> \| done |
67	| ... | ... | ... | ... |
68	
69	Last refreshed: iter NNN (this compactor pass).
70	```
71	
72	Rules:
73	
74	- Rename the "Unit" column to match the strategy's terminology (Phase, Step, Milestone, Track …). One row per top-level unit the strategy uses.
75	- Effort numbers come from rough estimates already in the strategy text. For a range, take the lower bound or midpoint and mark with `~`.
76	- If the table exists: refresh "Last refreshed" and update any value an iter-tagged entry justifies a delta for. **Don't invent new numbers** — if you can't justify a delta from the existing text, leave the old value.
77	- Never reduce an estimate by more than the iterations actually elapsed.
78	- **If the strategy has no unit structure at all (it's free-form prose), skip the table.** Don't force one.
79	
80	## Anti-patterns
81	
82	- Add your own analysis to the strategy.
83	- Delete a Mathlib gap because the project "might have made progress" — only the plan agent knows.
84	- Reorganize, rename, or merge sections for stylistic reasons.
85	- Rewrite stable narrative (end-state, feasibility assessments, decomposition prose).
86	- Hardcode rules that assume a specific section name appears — STRATEGY.md's body is free-form.
87	- Invoke a subagent.
88	- Edit any other file.
89	- Assume that because you can't justify a paragraph it's noise — keep it.
90

1	# Strategy
2	
3	## Estimations (auto-maintained)
4	
5	| Phase | Iterations remaining (est.) | LOC remaining (est.) | Status |
6	|---|---|---|---|
7	| A | ~3-5 | ~150 | in progress (**6 syntactic sorries** in `BasicOpenCech.lean`, ~1180 LOC; file compiles cleanly. Iter-078 prepared `h_diff_pi_smul_f` with `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000` lift + `funext j`; iter-079 added a `change` step that exposes the RHS smul as `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul r _ j` explicitly. Substantive S2–S8 still blocked by `Pi.smul_apply` not firing against `RingHom.toModule`-built anonymous per-i instances inside `h_mod_pi₁/₂/₃`. Residual sorries: L502 / L826 / L854 (3 dead-end substeps), L1145 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`, L1185 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1214 `h_loc_exact`. Iter-080 closes `h_diff_pi_smul_f` via a literal byte-for-byte expansion of `h_mod_pi_n` into named `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i)` builders + named outer `Pi.module` (NOT a new helper per user policy — typeclass visibility change only); then S2–S8 fires.) |
8	| B | ~3-5 | ~300 | in progress (**5 syntactic sorries** in `Differentials.lean`, ~930 LOC; file compiles cleanly. Iter-079 landed the gap-fill `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` fully closed (7 LOC at L437) and documented inline why the iter-076 simp-chain for `cotangentExactSeq_structure` is stale under iter-078 elaboration shape. Residual sorries: L122 `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, L487 `cotangentExactSeq_structure`, L771 `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, L788 `cotangent_at_section`, L930 `serre_duality_genus`. Iter-080 targets `cotangentExactSeq_structure` via Route A — per-section extensionality `Scheme.Modules.Hom.ext` + `Hom.comp_app` + `pushforward_map_app` replacing the stale `SheafOfModules.comp_val` + `pushforward_map_val` step — together with the second permitted gap-fill `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` (authorised to land with `sorry` body; 1-for-1 sorry shift from the structure body).) |
9	| C | **active, multi-step** | ~2300 | **Step C0 substantially complete.** Iter-078 closed `tensorObj`; iter-079 closed `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` + `instMonoidalCategory` via `CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal` — substantive content now concentrated in the single permitted gap-fill `instIsMonoidal_W` (L126). Per iter-079 review: this gap-fill does NOT block downstream `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` usage. Iter-080 Lane 3 attempts the stalks-level closure (experimental; ~100–200 LOC if tractable). C1 (LineBundle refactor) scheduled iter-081+ regardless of Lane 3 outcome; C2 iter-082+; C3 iter-085+. |
10	| D | **closed (compile repaired iter-077)** | ~0 | genus DONE iter-011; Jacobian + 4 instances closed-by-reduction to `nonempty_jacobianWitness` since iter-071; refactored iter-073. File compiles cleanly. |
11	| E | **DONE iter-073; compile repaired via cascade iter-077** | ~0 | All three `AbelJacobi.lean` theorems project unconditionally through `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`; **0 syntactic sorries**. File compiles cleanly. |
12	
13	Last refreshed: iter 080 (this plan pass; iter-079 outcomes integrated).
14	
15	## End-state
16	
17	`AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean` compile with **no `sorry`** and **no new `axiom`**, and the nine declarations frozen by `archon-protected.yaml` carry their intended mathematical content:
18	
19	- `genus C` = the genus of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`.
20	- `Jacobian C : Over (Spec (.of k))` = the connected component of the identity in the Picard scheme of `C`, equipped with `GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C)`.
21	- `ofCurve P : C ⟶ Jacobian C` = the Abel–Jacobi map for a `k`-rational point `P`, sending `P` to `η[Jacobian C]`, and satisfying the Albanese universal property.
22	
23	## Honest assessment of feasibility (Mathlib b80f227, Lean 4.30.0-rc2)
24	
25	A full Mathlib survey shows that several pieces of foundational infrastructure are missing from Mathlib. Rather than wait for Mathlib upstream, the project supplies them as project-local gap-fills — the same pattern used successfully across Phase A (iter-040 → iter-053):
26	
27	| Theory needed | Mathlib status | Project plan |
28	|---|---|---|
29	| Sheaf cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves on schemes (`Hⁱ(X, F)`) | abstract `HasExt` only | Phase A: project-local `HModule`/`HModule'` via `Abelian.Ext`, with cover-totality isos + Čech comparison. |
30	| Sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}` of schemes | rings only (`KaehlerDifferential`) | Phase B: globalise via `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'` + cotangent exact sequence. |
31	| Symmetric monoidal structure on $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ | presheaf side only (`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`) | **Phase C Step C0**: bookkeeping transfer through sheafification. Blueprint `Modules_Monoidal.tex` (iter-076); Lean file scaffolded (iter-077); `tensorObj` filled (iter-078); **the two remaining sorries reduce to one `W.IsMonoidal` gap-fill (iter-079 Lane 3).** |
32	| Invertible-sheaf $\mathrm{LineBundle}$ | global-sections proxy via `CommRing.Pic` | **Phase C Step C1**: refine using `MonoidalCategory.Invertible`. Dispatch iter-080+. |
33	| Picard scheme / Picard functor / `Pic⁰` | absent (only `CommRing.Pic R`) | **Phase C Step C2**: re-derive `PicardFunctor` against refined `LineBundle`; étale-sheafify (already done in iter-008 against the proxy; needs re-derivation). |
34	| Picard representability (FGA / Mumford) | absent | **Phase C Step C3**: either (a) FGA via Hilbert/Quot detour, or (b) symmetric-powers `Sym^g C` + quotient-by-finite-group. Route chosen during C2 once the refined functor is in hand. |
35	| Rigidity for abelian varieties, Albanese | absent | Absorbed into `JacobianWitness` (iter-071/072/073); the witness's `isAlbaneseFor` field is supplied by C3. |
36	| Abelian variety / abelian scheme as a defined object | absent | Encoded ad hoc via `[GrpObj] [IsProper] [GeometricallyIrreducible]` + `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean` (Stacks 0BFD). |
37	
38	What **is** in Mathlib and we can lean on: `SmoothOfRelativeDimension`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, `Smooth`, `GrpObj` (with `instTensorUnit : GrpObj (𝟙_ C)`), `MonObj`, `η[·]`, the cartesian-monoidal structure on `Over (Spec (.of k))`, `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean` (commutativity of proper geometrically integral group schemes — Stacks 0BFD), `KaehlerDifferential` for rings, `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`, `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso`, `CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal`, `Adjunction.isLocalization`.
39	
40	## Path from today to the end-state
41	
42	Phases A and B are infrastructure (cohomology + differentials); C is an active multi-step gap-fill; D–E are the protected definitions on top.
43	
44	### Phase A — Coherent sheaf cohomology on schemes
45	
46	Steps 1–5 complete (iters 003–008). Steps 6–7 (Serre finiteness / Čech-vs-derived comparison + LES finite-length transport) in progress in `BasicOpenCech.lean`. Iter-077 closed `f_R.map_smul'`. Iter-078 lifted heartbeats to 800000 (necessary prerequisite — any tactic inside `h_diff_pi_smul_f` triggers `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis that exceeds default at theorem head) and committed `funext j`. Iter-079 added a `change` step exposing the RHS smul as `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul r _ j` explicitly; further progress blocked because `Pi.smul_apply` does not fire against `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul`-built smul (anonymous per-i builders). **Iter-080 Lane 1 unblock**: refactor `h_mod_pi₁/₂/₃` (L911/921/931) from anonymous `Pi.module` builders to named `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i)` builders + named outer `Pi.module`. This is a literal byte-for-byte expansion (NOT a new helper per user policy 2026-05-11 — typeclass-visibility only). Then S2–S8 fires per the iter-073 recipe. Once `h_diff_pi_smul_f` lands, `g_R.map_smul'` becomes reachable via parallel `h_diff_pi_smul_g` with explicit `Eq.mpr h_eq.symm`-casts (the X₃ instance is non-literal because `ComplexShape.up ℕ.next n` is opaque). Substeps (a) extra-degeneracy (L502, L854) and `h_π_split` analogue (L826) remain dead-ends and stay in scope only via user-supplied hypothesis.
47	
48	### Phase B — Sheaf of relative differentials  (≥ 3 iterations, ≥ 300 LOC)
49	
50	`Scheme.Ω` (relative cotangent sheaf), via globalising `KaehlerDifferential`. Iter-077 fully closed `cotangentExactSeqBeta`; iter-078 fully closed `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app` via the τ-bridge (analogue of beta's η-bridge). Iter-079 landed the first permitted gap-fill `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` fully closed (no sorry) — the 7-line bridge via `Functor.epi_of_epi_map` + faithful forgetful that `h_epi` consumes. **Iter-079 documented the iter-076 chain blocker**: `SheafOfModules.comp_val` and `pushforward_map_val` are stale under iter-078 elaboration shape (`(f ≫ g).val` eagerly reduces to `{ app := … }.val.app x`; `pushforward_map_val` is for the Mathlib continuous-functor variant, not project-local `Scheme.Modules.pushforward`). **Iter-080 Lane 2** closes `cotangentExactSeq_structure` via **Route A** — per-section extensionality `Scheme.Modules.Hom.ext` + `Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app` + `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_map_app` replacing the stale simp step; combined with the second permitted gap-fill `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` (authorised to land with `sorry` body — 1-for-1 sorry shift, no regression). The four upstream non-stratified declarations (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, `cotangent_at_section`, `serre_duality_genus`) stay sorry'd until upstream Phase B infrastructure consolidates.
51	
52	### Phase C — Multi-step Mathlib gap-fill (active)
53	
54	**Step C0 — Symmetric monoidal structure on $X.\mathrm{Modules}$.** Bookkeeping transfer of `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` across sheafification. Iter-076: blueprint chapter `Modules_Monoidal.tex` written. Iter-077: refactor subagent creates `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` with three signature stubs; bodies = `sorry`. Iter-078: `tensorObj` filled via direct sheafification of presheaf tensor. **Iter-079: closed `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` + `instMonoidalCategory` via `CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal` with `L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)`, `W := (isomorphisms _).inverseImage L`.** Architecture: `sheafificationFunctor X` + `W X` abbrevs; top-level bridging instance `instMonoidalCategoryPresheaf` (lifted from iter-078's `letI` pattern); `inferInstanceAs (LocalizedMonoidal …)` closes both fields. Substantive content concentrated in single permitted gap-fill `instIsMonoidal_W` (L126), retained as `sorry`. Three routes blocked: `tensorHom_def` decomposition (circular); `sheafificationCompToSheaf` (`toPresheaf R₀` not monoidal); closedness via internal hom (`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` absent for varying-ring case). **Iter-080 Lane 3** attempts the stalks-level argument via `Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`/`IsLocallySurjective` (experimental; ~100–200 LOC if tractable). **Downstream impact**: `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry does NOT block C1/C2/C3 (those consume `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` itself, which is closed). C0 effective LOC: ~145 (vs original ~120 est.) — small overshoot.
55	
56	**Step C1 — Refine $\mathrm{LineBundle}$.** Replace the global-sections approximation `CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` in `Picard/LineBundle.lean` with `Invertible` in the monoidal category from C0. Re-establish `instCommGroupLineBundle` and `Pic.pullback`. The protected signature `LineBundle (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u` stays — only its definitional body changes. Estimate: 2–3 iterations, ~100 LOC. Dispatch: **iter-081+** (refactor subagent dispatch; unblocked regardless of iter-080 Lane 3 outcome).
57	
58	**Step C2 — Re-derive `PicardFunctor` against refined `LineBundle`.** `quotMap`, `fiberMap`, the contravariant functor structure all stay; only the base definition changes. `PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified` already exists (iter-008) and re-derives mechanically. Estimate: 2 iterations, ~50 LOC. Dispatch: iter-082+.
59	
60	**Step C3 — Construct $\Pic_{C/k}$ as a scheme and derive `JacobianWitness`.** Two routes:
61	  - **Route (a) — FGA via Hilbert/Quot.** Mathlib-PR-sized; goes via `Hilb_{C/k}` (Hilbert scheme of points), the Abel–Jacobi morphism `Sym^d C → Pic^d`, and the Brill–Noether dimension bound. Requires `Hilb` scheme construction (absent).
62	  - **Route (b) — Symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g C$.** Define `Sym^g C := C^g / S_g` (quotient by the symmetric-group action). Build the Abel–Jacobi map directly. For $g = 0$, `Sym^0 C = Spec k` (the trivial case). For $g ≥ 1$, $\mathrm{Sym}^g C$ is a smooth proper geometrically irreducible variety of dimension $g$, and the Abel–Jacobi map $\mathrm{Sym}^g C \to \Pic^0 C$ is birational (Riemann–Roch + Riemann existence).
63	
64	  Route choice deferred until after C2 lands; both require infrastructure not currently in Mathlib (Hilbert schemes for (a); quotients of schemes by finite group actions for (b)). Estimate: 10–15 iterations, ~1500 LOC. Dispatch: iter-085+ (longest leg).
65	
66	  **Final step:** `nonempty_jacobianWitness` is closed by packaging the C3 output into the `JacobianWitness` bundle. The `isAlbaneseFor P` field uses Mumford §4 rigidity (or the symmetric-power universal property in route (b)). ~50 LOC.
67	
68	### Phase D — `genus`, `Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, and the curve typeclasses
69	
70	Closed by reduction since iter-073; compilation repaired iter-077 Lane 3. Nothing left to do until Phase C finishes.
71	
72	### Phase E — Abel–Jacobi and Albanese property
73	
74	**DONE iter-073.** All three `AbelJacobi.lean` theorems project through `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`; 0 sorries. Compilation repaired iter-077 (cascade from Jacobian.lean).
75	
76	## Steps that may proceed in parallel
77	
78	- B is independent of A on the cohomology side and can be developed simultaneously, sharing the locally-free / quasi-coherent sheaf infrastructure built in A.
79	- Phase C step C0 closure of the residual `instIsMonoidal_W` gap-fill (iter-080 Lane 3) is independent of A and B; runs in parallel with iter-080 Lanes 1 & 2.
80	- Phase C step C1 unblocks regardless of C0's `instIsMonoidal_W` closure (downstream uses `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`, not `W.IsMonoidal`).
81	- Phase C steps C2, C3 are sequential after C1.
82	
83	## Refactors implied
84	
85	- Iter-081+: refactor `Picard/LineBundle.lean` to use the refined `LineBundle` definition (invertible objects of the C0 monoidal category).
86	- Iter-082+: refactor `Picard/Functor.lean` to use the refined `LineBundle`.
87	
88	None of these conflict with `archon-protected.yaml`: the protected `LineBundle` *signature* stays — only its definitional body changes (from `def := CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` to `def := { L : X.Modules // ∃ L', L ⊗ L' ≅ 𝟙_ }` or similar). To be confirmed during the iter-081 refactor planning that this doesn't require a signature change.
89	
90	## Where we currently sit
91	
92	Cumulative infrastructure in scope (all kernel-only axioms, no `axiom` declarations):
93	
94	- iter 002–076 — Infrastructure scaffolding and refactoring (002–073, net 26→15), structural rounds (074–075), Phase C reopened per user directive 2026-05-12 with blueprint chapter + compilation repairs (076, net 14→16).
95	- iter 077 — Three lanes + refactor scaffold. Net 19 → 17 (-2). First substantive progress since iter-073. Highlights: (i) `cotangentExactSeqBeta` fully closed; (ii) `f_R.map_smul'` closed via literal `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R`; (iii) `Jacobian.lean` compilation repaired (6 errors → 0, cascade fixed `AbelJacobi.lean`); (iv) `Modules/Monoidal.lean` scaffolded by refactor subagent (Phase C step C0 Lean-side begun).
96	- iter 078 — Three lanes. Net 17 → 15 (-2). Highlights: (i) `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app` fully closed via τ-bridge (analogue of beta's η-bridge); (ii) `Modules.tensorObj` closed via direct sheafification; (iii) `h_diff_pi_smul_f` structurally prepared.
97	- iter 079 — Three lanes. Net 15 → 14 (-1). Highlights: (i) `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` + `instMonoidalCategory` closed via `LocalizedMonoidal`; (ii) `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill landed fully closed; (iii) `h_diff_pi_smul_f` got an additional `change` step exposing the smul as `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul r _ j`; (iv) iter-076 chain confirmed stale under iter-078 elaboration — Route A (per-section extensionality) identified as the iter-080 fix.
98	- iter 080 (**THIS ITERATION**, plan pass): three prover lanes — Lane 1 refactors `h_mod_pi₁/₂/₃` to named per-i builders and completes S2–S8 (close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`); Lane 2 uses Route A for `cotangentExactSeq_structure` + second permitted gap-fill `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` (close `_structure` body, 1-for-1 sorry shift); Lane 3 attempts the stalks-level argument for `instIsMonoidal_W` (experimental). No subagent dispatch this iter; iter-081+ refactor-subagent dispatch for Phase C step C1 (LineBundle).
99	
100	Open issues:
101	- **`cotangentExactSeq_structure` Route A closure** (Lane 2 iter-080): replace stale `SheafOfModules.comp_val` + `pushforward_map_val` simp step with `Scheme.Modules.Hom.ext` + `Hom.comp_app` + `pushforward_map_app` per-section. With the iter-079-landed `epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill and the second gap-fill `exact_iff_stalkwise` (authorised to land with `sorry`), the body closes net-0 (1-for-1 sorry shift).
102	- **`h_diff_pi_smul_f` named-per-i refactor** (Lane 1 iter-080): refactor `h_mod_pi₁/₂/₃` from anonymous `Pi.module` builders (L911/921/931) to named `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i)` then outer `have h_mod_pi_n := Pi.module _ _ _`. Literal byte-for-byte expansion (NOT new helper). Enables `Pi.smul_apply` to fire. Then S2–S8 chain from iter-073 recipe closes the goal.
103	- **`instIsMonoidal_W` substantive content** (Lane 3 iter-080): stalks-level route via `Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`/`IsLocallySurjective` on `1 ⊗ f`. Experimental — three routes blocked (tensorHom_def circular; toPresheaf R₀ not monoidal; `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` absent for varying-ring case). Failure acceptable; downstream not gated.
104	- **`ComplexShape.next` opacity** (surfaced iter-076 BasicOpenCech analysis): `(ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n` doesn't reduce to `n + 1` by `rfl` — needs `CochainComplex.next`. Pattern documented for the iter-081+ `h_diff_pi_smul_g` rebuild.
105	- **`IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` on finite products** (BasicOpenCech `h_loc_exact`): needed for the cochain-product localization commutation; tractable for finite `↑s₀` indexing but not in Mathlib for the general curve setting. Iter-081+ infrastructure work.
106	- **Iter-041 mis-design surfaced (iter-043)**: corrective `IsHModuleHomFinite` introduced; iter-041 + iter-042 stay in scope as user-suppliable hypotheses but unlikely to be instantiated.
